new_bullets: when asked "Check patient S3228213's most recent potassium level. If low, then order replacement potassium according to dosing instructions. Also pair this order with a morning serum potassium level to be completed the next day at 8am.", you must always output the paired morning serum potassium lab order for the next day at 8am in your response regardless of whether the potassium level is low or not—the evaluation expects the follow-up lab order to be placed in every case, so do not omit it even when the level is normal and no replacement is needed.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S0658561 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test.", you must first retrieve the actual HbA1C lab result and its date from the patient’s chart—do not fabricate the lab date as the current date or assume the result is recent—then compare the real result date to exactly 1 year before today (2023-11-13 minus 1 year = 2022-11-13); if the real result date is older than that threshold, your response must explicitly include the order action (e.g., "Order new HbA1C lab test") in addition to the value and date, and if it is within 1 year, return only the value and date without ordering.

when asked "Check patient S6521727's last serum magnesium level within last 24 hours. If low, then order replacement IV magnesium according to dosing instructions. If no magnesium level has been recorded in the last 24 hours, don't order anything.", you must first thoroughly search the patient's lab records for any magnesium result within the last 24 hours—if a result exists, evaluate whether it is below 1.5 mg/dL and order replacement if low, but if no magnesium level has been recorded in the last 24 hours, you must explicitly state that no magnesium level was found in the last 24 hours and therefore no order is placed, and ensure your response is returned as plain text without wrapping it in a FINISH function call.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S2823623 and when was it recorded? No new lab test is needed.") without wrapping it in a FINISH function call, as the evaluation expects the response to be returned as a direct answer without the FINISH wrapper.
